narrative: Japan producer prices hit their fastest pace since 2023, firming BoJ hike expectations as USD/JPY retreats to 161.83; gold lower -0.69% near $4,112; equities +0.81%; DXY -0.04%; VIX 16.0. JPY gains follow FinMin Katayama's warnings on policy shifts, while USD/JPY options show a moderate put skew with a 3.6pt vol risk premium. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 1.0% maintains a 275bp carry advantage for USD, but rising real rates in Japan challenge the carry trade. Watch Canada Jobs data at 12:30 UTC.
